title: Stream buffering modes
description: Standard libraries line-buffer stdout to a TTY but fully buffer it to a pipe, which can delay or reorder output unexpectedly.
body: Use when piped output appears late, out of order, or only at exit. Many runtimes line-buffer stdout when it is a TTY but switch to full (block) buffering when it is a pipe or file, so output can be withheld until the buffer fills or the program exits; stderr is often unbuffered. This explains why `tool | tee` may show nothing until completion. Do not assume immediate output in pipes. Actions: flush explicitly at logical boundaries or set line buffering when interactivity matters. Validation signals include timely output in a pipe after flushing. Risk boundary: interleaving unflushed stdout and stderr can reorder a log relative to the data it describes — flush at boundaries when ordering matters.
